title: 怪屋
summary:
Edith de Havilland explains to Charles Haywood that Aristide Leonidis was so wealthy that his family members, including Roger and Sophia, were financially independent and lacked an immediate motive to kill him for inheritance. She suggests that Aristide's much younger wife, Brenda, might have poisoned him because she grew tired of waiting for him to die, though Edith notes that Aristide was a strong man who showed no signs of declining health. Observing Edith's calculating demeanor, Charles considers whether she herself might be a suspect.

Edith leads Charles to the home of her brother-in-law, Philip Leonidis, a landmark building in London. After passing through an austere hall and a blue-paneled drawing room decorated with stage photographs and a Degas painting, they enter a cold, book-filled library. There, Charles meets Philip, a handsome, calm man around fifty who does not smoke. Philip appears listless and remains unemotional regarding the murder.

The meeting is interrupted by the arrival of Margda Leonidis, who enters the library wearing a peach satin nightgown and smoking a cigarette. Speaking rapidly and loudly, Margda expresses anxiety regarding the public attention the murder has attracted and discusses her desire for freedom and potential acting roles inspired by the tragedy. During her frantic monologue, she drops a cigarette butt onto Philip's mahogany desk, which he removes without comment. Margda concludes by feigning a terrified expression, claiming the situation is too terrible to think about.
